beats.title: It started with FRC.
beats.body: Grade-school me watched 120-pound robots launch foam rings across a field and decided I had to build one. That first season taught me more than any class.

beats.title: Then came CAD.
beats.body: OnShape became a second IDE. Part studios, mate connectors, feature scripts — modeling a mechanism before it ever touched aluminum saved weeks.

beats.title: Iterate. Break. Iterate.
beats.body: Every subsystem got three revisions minimum. Intake arms, climbing linkages, swerve modules — the rule: fail fast on paper, ship what survives.

beats.title: Now I lead the build.
beats.body: Captaining FTC 8569 RoboKnights and mentoring 22377 SigmaCorns means the learning loop goes both ways — and the robots only get better.
